response.justification: This candidate brings well over the required 6 years of full-stack experience, with a clear and recent TypeScript/Node.js focus across production systems at scale (millions of mails, millions of HTTP requests/second). The AI agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at their most recent role directly addresses the AI agent development requirement, which is a differentiating and often hard-to-find skill. Testing culture is unusually strong — not just practiced but taught and tooled (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Selenium courses, TDD advocacy). Primary gaps are NestJS (the specific backend framework listed) and Kotlin (the secondary JVM language), neither of which is disqualifying given the demonstrated pattern of rapidly adopting new frameworks across the career. Vitest is also unmentioned but given the depth of testing experience, it is a low adoption-risk gap.
strengths: Deep, current TypeScript/Node.js expertise demonstrated across multiple production environments handling significant scale, directly aligned with the primary stack
Hands-on AI agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling experience at current role — a rare and directly relevant match to the AI agent development requirement
Exceptionally strong testing-first culture: created testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran TDD/Selenium workshops, and applied TDD/CI/CD practices professionally across multiple teams
concerns: NestJS not mentioned anywhere on the resume despite being the primary backend framework in the job's stack — unclear whether candidate has exposure or would need a ramp-up period
Kotlin is absent; only Java appears and only in older roles — the JVM secondary stack requirement is weakly covered
Vitest not referenced; testing framework experience is demonstrated primarily through custom tooling and Selenium, leaving alignment with the specific testing stack unverified

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
